# South Sudan flood — `ssd-flood`

In May 2022 CERF released US$15 million anticipatorily — months earlier than the 2020 and 2021 flood responses — ahead of the projected 2022 rainy-season floods in South Sudan, complemented by US$4 million from the South Sudan Humanitarian Fund (SSHF). The funds concentrated on the Bentiu IDP camp and surrounding areas in Unity State, where more than 320,000 people faced a high risk of flooding. Crucially, this framework had **no formal quantitative trigger**: OCHA explored available forecasts in March 2022 and concluded they were not reliable enough to calibrate one, so the anticipatory decision was made on risk analysis and expert judgement. Partners constructed and reinforced over 55 km of dykes and invested early in WASH and cholera prevention, reaching 301,649 people and reportedly preventing the evacuation of around 100,000 people. It was a single-season framework and has not since been renewed as a recurring, trigger-based one.

**Current version:** [2022-05](2022-05.md) · status: **retired**
